title: Lively Peppery Australian Shrimp Stir-fry
description: A festive, peppery stir-fry featuring shrimp, wok-hei sauce and creamy soy milk for a gooey, pescatarian-friendly dish.
instructions: Heat a wok over high heat until smoking and swirl sesame oil inside.
Add minced garlic, grated ginger, and chili flakes; stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Toss in sliced red and green bell peppers; cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Insert shrimp and stir until they just start to turn pink, about 1 minute.
Pour in soy milk and wok-hei seasoning sauce, bring to a gentle simmer.
Stir in soy sauce and add the cornstarch slurry; cook until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy, about 2 minutes.
Add sliced cucumber, stir quickly for 30 seconds to preserve crunch.
Taste and adjust seasoning with more soy sauce or a pinch of salt if needed.
Transfer the stir-fry to a plate; drizzle with a few drops of sesame oil for shine.
Serve immediately over steamed rice or noodles.
